document.title: Risk Management Plan for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector using EN ISO 14971:2012 and related medical device standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines the risk management process, scope, responsibilities, acceptance criteria, and post-production information handling for ADD during its life cycle.
keyPoints: The plan applies risk management to the entire life cycle of ADD, including plan, development, design and production, and post-production information management.
Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2012.
The intended use is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, excluding mammography.
All risks are to be reduced as far as possible without economic considerations.
Post-production information is collected from internal and external sources such as PMCF, CAPA reports, complaints, recalls, literature, similar devices, and law or standards trends.

concepts.name: Intended use

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity multiplied by probability.
